reasoning:
The current TGA balance (378B as of June 4) is drastically lower than historical averages (~750B) due to the reinstatement of the debt ceiling on January 2, 2025, which forces the Treasury to spend down its cash instead of issuing net new debt. 

For the June 15 resolution (week ending June 11): The debt ceiling drain will continue. Because mid-June corporate tax receipts aren't due until June 16, the TGA is virtually guaranteed to decrease further from its June 4 level. (p=0.20)

For the July 8 resolution (week ending July 2): Mid-June tax receipts will temporarily boost the TGA, but the ongoing debt limit structural drain and the major July 1 start-of-month government payouts will likely pull the balance back down to around or below the 378B mark. (p=0.35)

For the Sept 6 resolution (week ending Sept 3): By late summer, the Treasury will hit the X-date, forcing Congress to raise the debt limit. Historically, this is immediately followed by a barrage of bill issuance that refits the TGA to its ~750B target, which strongly supports an increase above 378B. However, the new administration's focus on government efficiency (DOGE) creates a significant possibility that the Treasury permanently lowers its TGA target to reduce interest expenses. (p=0.60)

For Dec 5: Further out, extreme policy unpredictability around future TGA structural targets warrants a probability closer to the 0.5 uninformative prior, fully complying with standard long-term uncertainty scaling. (p=0.55)
